title: cassinia leptophylla
intro: Cassinia leptophylla, also known as needle bush, is a small shrub native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Cassinia leptophylla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile remedy for various health issues.
